HEAVY RAINFALL WHICH APPROACHED 3 TO 4 INCHES IN 3 HOURS PRODUCED WIDESPREAD STREET FLOODING... FLOODING OF UNDERPASSES... STORM SEWER BACKUP... AND BASEMENT FLOODING ACROSS THE FARGO METRO AREA. RIVER LEVELS IN TOWN WERE AREADY ABOVE MINOR FLOOD STAGE DUE TO PREVIOUS HEAVY RAINFALL IN THE AREA... WHICH MEANT THAT STORM SEWER FLAP GATES WERE CLOSED AND PUMPING STATIONS WERE LIKELY OVERWHELMED BY THE HIGH LOCAL RUNOFF RATES.

A TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR ABOUT 7 MILES... FROM ABOUT A MILE WEST OF THE AIRPORT TO THE INTERSTATE 94 EXIT ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF TOWN BY 2230 PM CDT. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO AROUND 4 MILES NE OF TOWN BY 2338 PM CDT. THE TORNADO WAS LIKELY WRAPPED IN HEAVY RAIN DURING PORTIONS OF THIS TRACK. ONE LIVESTOCK LOAFING SHED WAS LIFTED AND COLLAPSED AND SEVERAL TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS AND PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH.

SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE IN THIEF RIVER FALLS. NUMEROUS ROOFS DAMAGED...MOTOR HOME FLIPPED...BILLBOARDS FROM EAST SIDE OF TOWN AND OUT OF TOWN ARE BENT OR BROKEN. APPEARS MAJORITY OF WIND DAMAGE IS IN A 1/4 MILE WIDE PATH. WIND APPEARS TO HAVE BEEN OUT OF THE NORTHWEST. STREET FLOODING WIDESPREAD IN TOWN AS WELL. TIME APPROXIMATE.

TORNADO DAMAGE PATH LENGTH WAS AN INTERMITTEN 18 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 20 TO 150 YARDS FROM 4 ESE OF MAHNOMEN TO 4 MILES WNW OF ZERKEL FROM APPROXIMATELY 150AM TO 230AM. DAMAGE PEAKED AT AN EF2 RATING WITH WIND SPEEDS IN THE 115 TO 120 MPH RANGE. THIS WAS INDICATED WHERE A POLE BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND FARM EQUIPMENT LOFTED.

REPORTS FROM LAW ENFORCEMENT INDICATE A BAND OF WIND DAMAGE FROM 8 E MAHNOMEN ALONG 230TH ST WHERE A FARMSTED HAD QUONSET DAMAGE EAST TO THE ROY LAKE AREA ALONG THE CLEARWATER COUNTY LINE WHERE TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWN. POSSIBLE ROOF DAMAGE TO HOME IN AREA. EARLY INDICATIONS FROM LAW ENFORCEMENT WAS STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.
NUMEROUS TREE DAMAGE ALONG WITH SOME TIN ROOFING PEELED OFF OF A SHED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WINDS BLEW DOWN 2 LARGE OAK TREES AND 7 COTTONWOOD TREES TEMPORARILY BLOCKING ROAD. THERE WAS A SIGNIFICANT AMOUNT OF 1/4 TO 1/2 INCH HAIL...SUFFICIENT TO DRIFT AND REMAIN ON THE GROUND TILL EVENING.
SHINGLES BLOWN OFF RESIDENTS ROOF. NUMEROUS MEDIUM TREES BLOWN DOWN INCLUDING PINES AND POPPLERS. SIGNIFICANT 1/4 TO 1/2 INCH HAIL COVERED THE GROUND FOR A PERIOD OF TIME AFTER STORM PASSED.

A LARGE WIND DRIVEN HAIL SWATH DEVELOPED ACROSS SOUTHERN COLFAX-WEST TOWNSHIP AND NORTHERN NANSEN TOWNSHIP. IN SEVERAL SECTIONS OF FARMLAND CROPS WERE SHATTERED DOWN TO STEMS AND STUMPS.

A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED NEAR A FARMSTEAD AND BROKE MUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WHICH THEN DAMAGED AN OCCUPIED TRACTOR CAB AND NEARBY FARM OUTBUILDINGS. THIS TORNADO APPEARED TO BECOME QUICKLY ENGULFED IN DRIVING RAIN AND HAIL. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED 100 YARDS. TRACK LENGTH LESS THAN ONE HALF MILE AND ENDING BY 414 PM CDT. ORIGINAL REPORT RELAYED THROUGH COLFAX FIRE DEPT.
A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OVER OPEN COUNTRY WAS RELAYED THROUGH COLFAX FIRE DEPT.

A PARTIALLY RAIN AND HAIL WRAPPED TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO AROUND 3 S OF GALCHUTT BY 431 PM CDT. THE TORNADO SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES IN A SERIES OF SHELTER BELTS AND THREW LARGE TREE LIMBS WELL INTO THE FIELDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 90 MPH WITH MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY CROSSED INTERSTATE 29 NEAR MP 29 DURING THIS PERIOD.

A SWATH OF LARGE HAIL AND DAMAGING WINDS TO 70 MPH PRODUCED WIDESPREAD TREE AND CROP DAMAGE ACROSS CENTRAL DWIGHT-WEST TOWNSHIP. IN THE TOWN OF DWIGHT... A FEW LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASE WHILE LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN OFF THROUGHOUT THE TOWN.
HAIL UP TO HEN EGG SIZED OCCURRED FOR AT LEAST 5 MINUTES... ENDING AROUND 445 PM CDT.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. POWER POLES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWN. A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF OR COLLAPSED ON A STEEL BUILDING.

WIND DRIVEN HAIL PRODUCED WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE ACROSS NORTHERN AND EASTERN NEIGHBORHOODS IN WAHPETON. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES OR TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED OFF NEAR GROUND LEVEL WHILE NEIGHBORHOODS WERE LITTERED WITH SMALLER BRANCHES...LEAF DEBRIS AND HAIL. PEAK WINDS ARE ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH THOUGH STRONG TO DAMAGING WINDS LIKELY LASTED FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.
LARGE HAIL OCCURING... BUT UNABLE TO ESTIMATE SIZE AT THIS TIME.

NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. POWER POLES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWN. AN ENTIRE STEEL ROOF WAS TORN OFF ITS RAFTERS AND FLUNG SOUTHEASTWARD ACROSS MINNESOTA AVENUE. IMPACTS FROM THE DEBRIS BROKE DOWN A STEEL STREET LIGHT POLE AND CAUSED INCIDENTAL DAMAGE TO OTHER BUILDING ACROSS THE STREET. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH... WITH STRONG TO DAMAGING WINDS LASTING FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.
